title: Event time versus processing time
description: Why event time gives deterministic results and how watermarks track progress through event time.
body: Use when reasoning about which clock a streaming computation should follow. Event time is when an event actually occurred; processing time is when the engine handled it. Do not aggregate on processing time when correctness under replay matters — replays and delays shift processing time but not event time. Watermarks are the engine's estimate that no event earlier than time T remains, letting event-time windows close. Actions: assign event timestamps and watermarks, and reason about completeness via the watermark. Validation signals include reproducible aggregates across replays and watermark-driven window firing. Risk boundary: a stalled watermark blocks all event-time output; if windows freeze, escalate to inspect watermark progress before assuming missing data.
